title: arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. sensitiva
intro: Arctostaphylos uva-ursi subsp. sensitiva, also known as bearberry, is a subspecies of a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America and Europe. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high concentration of arbutin, a compound with antibacterial and antifungal properties, as well as its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of bearberry include its ability to treat urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health issues. Bearberry is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as kidney stones, cystitis, and prostatitis.
